Title: The LCG1-OsBP5/OsEBP89-Wx module regulates the grain chalkiness and taste quality in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SLR1-like/DELLA-related protein; rice SLRL2 orthologous to SLR1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in filling caryopsis; predominantly at 5, 10, and 15 DAP, then declines
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in early-developing caryopsis, especially dorsal vascular bundles, endosperm, cross cells, and outer pericarp at 5–15 DAP
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of rice eating and cooking quality; contributes to chalkiness-related grain quality differences
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LCG1 reduces grain chalkiness and improves taste quality by restraining amylose accumulation and coordinating starch, protein, and lipid deposition during grain filling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Map-based cloning identified LOC_Os05g49930 as LCG1, the causal locus reducing chalkiness in the Nipponbare introgression line. 2. LCG1 knockout increased chalky grain percentage and degree, increased amylose, protein and lipid content, reduced total starch, altered amylopectin chain-length distribution and starch granule packing, and worsened taste quality. 3. LCG1 physically interacts with OsBP5 and inhibits OsBP5 accumulation and OsBP5/OsEBP89-mediated activation of Wx, thereby lowering Wx expression. 4. Natural promoter variation elevates LCG1 expression in japonica haplotypes and is associated with lower chalkiness and better quality.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive); native LCG1 promoter/genomic fragment; LCG1 promoter for GUS reporter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; complementation; overexpression; qRT-PCR; GUS staining; immunofluorescence; subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; split-LUC; dual-luciferase reporter assay; metabolite/storage component profiling; SEM; amylopectin chain-length analysis; haplotype analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field conditions in Chengdu and Lingshui; grain filling/developmental stages 5–25 DAP
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome analysis of 6-DAP caryopsis; accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive 35S overexpression reduced plant height and secondary branch number and unexpectedly increased chalkiness; precise spatiotemporal expression appears necessary
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Elite promoter haplotypes Hap3/Hap5 and the Nipponbare LCG1 allele are useful for improving indica rice chalkiness and eating quality; marker-assisted introgression and near-isogenic deployment are suggested; avoid constitutive overexpression and instead tune native expression during early caryopsis development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sequence polymorphisms are mainly in the promoter and likely alter cis-elements; the favorable effect is associated with higher endogenous expression in japonica rather than non-specific overexpression

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose synthesis branch in endosperm starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate mostly linear α-1,4-glucan chains, producing amylose in starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step/terminal determinant for amylose accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines rice texture, viscosity, and eating quality; higher amylose is associated with poorer taste quality and increased chalkiness in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing caryopsis/endosperm; tested at 10, 15, and 20 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in LCG1-KO caryopsis; activated by OsBP5/OsEBP89 in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Wxb promoter (native promoter reporter in dual-luciferase assays)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher Wx expression promotes amylose accumulation and is associated with poorer taste quality and greater chalkiness-related deterioration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx was the most strongly upregulated starch synthesis gene in LCG1 mutants during early grain filling. 2. OsBP5 and OsEBP89 activated the Wxb promoter, while LCG1 inhibited this activation. 3. Elevated Wx expression in LCG1 loss-of-function plants correlated with increased amylose content and reduced eating quality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in developing caryopsis; dual-luciferase reporter assays in tobacco and rice protoplasts; grain amylose measurement; mutant phenotyping in LCG1 background
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing caryopsis during grain filling, especially 10–20 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome analysis of 6-DAP caryopsis; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression increases amylose content and worsens taste quality; excessive activation may contribute to higher chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning Wx expression, rather than simply maximizing or minimizing it, is valuable for achieving target amylose (~15%) and improving eating quality in Wx(b)-background rice; LCG1-based modulation offers an upstream tuning strategy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper studies Wx mainly as the direct metabolic output node of the LCG1-OsBP5/OsEBP89 regulatory module rather than as a newly discovered focal gene

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amylose biosynthesis via transcriptional control of Wx
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of rice texture and eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of Wx; protein accumulation is negatively affected by LCG1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Wx, especially together with OsEBP89
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: LCG1 protein interaction negatively affects OsBP5 protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator complex component controlling Wx expression and thereby amylose level
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tested in transient assays and protein interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Wxb promoter reporter used to assay transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsBP5 promotes Wx expression and amylose synthesis; its activity is antagonized by LCG1, contributing to lower chalkiness and better taste quality
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBP5 was identified as an LCG1-interacting protein by yeast two-hybrid screening and confirmed by BiFC and split-LUC. 2. Co-expression with LCG1 reduced OsBP5 protein accumulation. 3. OsBP5, together with OsEBP89, activated the Wxb promoter, and this activation was inhibited by LCG1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; co-localization; BiFC; split-LUC; protein accumulation assay in tobacco; dual-luciferase reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in tobacco leaves and rice protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As a positive activator of Wx, elevated OsBP5 activity would be expected to raise amylose and potentially reduce eating quality in this context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning amylose via upstream transcriptional control, but direct manipulation may risk excessive Wx activation; modulation through LCG1 may be more balanced
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Co-immunoprecipitation validation was unsuccessful; interaction support comes from Y2H, BiFC, split-LUC, and colocalization

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amylose biosynthesis via transcriptional control of Wx
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines rice eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of OsBP5/OsEBP89 complex
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Functionally antagonized by LCG1 through inhibition of the OsBP5/OsEBP89 complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential co-activator within the complex activating Wx and thus amylose synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Wxb promoter reporter used for transactivation tests
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsEBP89 cooperates with OsBP5 to activate Wx, promoting amylose synthesis; LCG1 suppresses this effect
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsEBP89 functioned with OsBP5 to activate the Wxb promoter in tobacco and protoplast luciferase assays. 2. Addition of LCG1 significantly inhibited the transcriptional activation of the OsBP5/OsEBP89 complex on Wx. 3. Thus OsEBP89 is a core component of the regulatory module controlling amylose-associated grain quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase reporter assays in tobacco leaves and rice protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression systems in tobacco and rice protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced OsEBP89 complex activity would be expected to elevate Wx expression and amylose, potentially impairing eating quality in this study context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory lever for amylose tuning, though likely best managed indirectly via LCG1 or combinatorial quality breeding strategies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsEBP89 was not newly discovered here, but it is experimentally integral to the core LCG1-OsBP5/OsEBP89-Wx module described in the Results
